titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Louis Choris Paintings and Sketches
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3151287.
This collection contains 44 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 61 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 13, 2023 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Louis Choris Paintings and Sketches

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Manuscript captions in French.

did.abstract: Watercolor paintings and sketches by Louis Choris of the Aleutian Islands, Alaska, the Bering Sea and the Alaskan Natives living on those lands, as well as other Arctic peoples including Chukchi and Itelmens in Russia. Made on the Kotzebue Expedition, 1815-1818.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Louis Choris (1795-1828) was a German-Russian painter and explorer.

In 1816, he visited the Pacific and the west coast of North America on board the Russian expeditionary ship Rurik , serving as an artist with the Romanzoff expedition under the command of Lieutenant Otto von Kotzebue (1787-1846), which was tasked with exploring a northwest passage.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Louis Choris Paintings and Sketches. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
Finding aid created from previously unpublished list housed with the collection. Titles in French have been translated into English. Supplied titles are in brackets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Watercolor paintings and sketches by Louis Choris of the Aleutian Islands, Alaska, the Bering Sea and the Alaskan Natives living on those lands, as well as other Arctic peoples including Chukchi and Itelmens in Russia. Made on the Kotzebue Expedition, 1815-1818.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Louis Choris Paintings and Sketches is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The entire collection is available in digital form.
